title: Baphiastrum pilosum
taxonomy: Baphiastrum pilosum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae, which is a large and diverse group of plants. Within the subfamily, Baphiastrum pilosum is placed in the tribe Dalbergieae, a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Baphiastrum is thought to be closely related to the genus Dalbergia, and the two are sometimes considered to be part of the same group. Further research is needed to confirm the exact relationships between Baphiastrum pilosum and other related species.

intro: Baphiastrum pilosum is a plant species belonging to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as a type of legume or bean plant. Traditional uses of Baphiastrum pilosum suggest it possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Baphiastrum pilosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Baphiastrum pilosum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, or tinctures to support wound healing, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
